Related, but different, modern programming is a lot less about syntax and a lot more about knowing the names and differences between tools– a lot less about how you hammer a nail and a lot more about whether you use a nailgun

Now us coders and programmers look like idiots; the robot can write the code easily, but people who have studied art history can easily triangulate their desired output between the names of 3 obscure artists; that is where the real wizardry is
The robot knows everything and to summon the correct magic you need to communicate your desires in a word or two
Knowing the names of things is the key to our AI future, as Le Guin foretold

In street photography all this obsession with "capturing" the "decisive moment" leads to this false perception that great photographers are extremely quick, much of their skill lying in the length of time between seeing the shot and closing the shutter
